role_summary: Civil rights movement participant; co-subject of a joint oral history with his wife Satoko Ito Ackerman (catalog entry 135).
ai_reading: David Mercer Ackerman shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 135 (catalog slug 'david-mercer-ackerman-and-satoko-ito-ackerman'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: David Mercer Ackerman's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript. David Mercer Ackerman is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 135 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for David Mercer Ackerman; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 135
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.caption: The Edmund Pettus Bridge in Selma, Alabama, on the route of the 1965 Selma to Montgomery march that Ackerman recalls joining.
